name: mcp_api_lan_thin_client_20260423
outcome_goal: Local-network thin clients can authenticate to the Praxis API and HTTP MCP bridge with the same operator API bearer token, while workflow-scoped MCP session tokens remain constrained to their allowed tools.
anti_requirements: Do not use SSH as the operator login path.
Do not create a second MCP authority.
Do not weaken workflow-scoped MCP session tokens.
Do not rely on localhost Postgres as operator authority.

jobs.label: mcp_api_lan_auth_bridge
jobs.prompt: Make the advertised API/MCP operator surface interchangeable for local-network thin clients. The HTTP MCP bridge should accept the same configured PRAXIS_API_TOKEN bearer token for ordinary operator MCP tools, while still preserving signed workflow-token scope for job/session tools. Add the missing workflow orient CLI alias so the catalog-advertised orient entrypoint exists.
